questions.question: What is the time complexity of Bubble Sort for an already sorted list?
questions.explanation: Bubble sort has a time complexity of O(n) for an already sorted list because it only needs to traverse the list once to confirm the sorted order.

questions.question: Which of the following data structures is suitable for performing Selection Sort?
questions.explanation: Selection sort is a suitable data structure for Linked Lists because it only needs to traverse the list once to find the minimum element.

questions.question: What is the average time complexity of Insertion Sort?
questions.explanation: The average time complexity of Insertion Sort is O(n^2) because it performs a series of comparisons and shifts to insert elements into the correct position.

questions.question: Which of the following sorting algorithms is not suitable for Sorting a list of objects?
questions.explanation: Bubble Sort is not suitable for sorting a list of objects because it only compares adjacent elements and does not take into account the properties of the objects.

questions.question: What is a common advantage of Insertion Sort?
questions.explanation: Insertion Sort is a stable sorting algorithm, which means that the order of equal elements is preserved during the sorting process.
